failure_modes.text: Facts stay disconnected and hard to retrieve when learners never build the map that lets details hang together on a usable structure.
failure_modes.source_quote: Facts must hang together on a latticework of theory.
failure_modes.mitigation: Teach the organizing structure before the details: give learners the map first so new facts have a place to attach, rather than accumulating disconnected knowledge.

failure_modes.text: A familiar schema can become a trap when it gets applied automatically to a novel problem that does not fit its assumptions.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the schema becomes a substitute for re-seeing reality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit schema fitness: when applying a familiar framework to a new problem, check whether the schema's assumptions still hold — if not, build a new one rather than force-fitting.

failure_modes.text: Framework soup can replace judgment when teams accumulate abstract models faster than they internalize their real purpose.
failure_modes.mitigation: Limit framework intake to what can be practiced: for each new model introduced, require a concrete application exercise before adding another, to prevent abstract accumulation without internalization.

premortem_questions.text: What map or analogy would let this new idea connect to something the learner already understands?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when new material must connect to prior knowledge.

premortem_questions.text: Where are we using a familiar template to classify the case quickly instead of checking whether it really fits this reality?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the schema becomes a substitute for re-seeing reality.

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe to accept a different schema that contradicts the one we are using now?

heuristics.text: Use chunking, scaffolding, and analogy so new material can attach to prior knowledge and become retrievable later.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when new material must connect to prior knowledge.

heuristics.text: Build a stable map that compresses the recommendation enough to guide later decisions instead of leaving people with scattered details.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when leaders need usable mental compression.

heuristics.text: Mobilize multiple frames when a familiar schema is dominating too early and may be blocking a better diagnosis.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on map-building, chunking, analogy, and anti-entrenchment rather than on broad knowledge-management commentary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor AI-persona or framework-catalog sprawl because the raw source already gives stronger schema-formation mechanics.
open_questions: The source contains rich communication examples, but the intervention layer stays closer to schema formation and misuse.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on cognitive-load-theory, analogical transfer, and beginner-minded design work.
antagonists: The source is strongest against availability-style substitution, confirmation-bias, and inside-view entrenchment.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit structured tensions around representativeness-bias, first-principles-thinking, and anchoring.
